page: Microsoft Azure EC2as_v5 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the ECASv5 server family. It is powered by an AMD processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The configuration provides 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU.

This server type does not include local storage, meaning all storage operations rely on external volumes. It is, however, designated as Premium Storage capable, allowing integration with high-performance remote disk options. There are zero integrated GPUs, making it unsuitable for hardware-accelerated graphics or parallel computing workloads. Additionally, it does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the EC2as_v5 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8, categorizing it as a memory-optimized resource. This density allows for efficient handling of memory-bound tasks without paying for unnecessary compute cores. The hardware profile is configured for workloads such as small-scale databases, caching layers, memory-intensive enterprise applications, and lightweight data processing tasks that require a larger memory footprint relative to processing power.
description: Microsoft Azure EC2as_v5 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine within the ECASv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an AMD processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory, establishing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, though it is Premium Storage capable for external disk configurations. It also lacks complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The high memory density relative to its compute capacity makes this server type a cost-efficient option for memory-bound workloads that do not require extensive CPU scaling. It is structured for workloads such as small-scale databases, in-memory caching, and lightweight enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure EC2as_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated AMD vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and Premium Storage capability, optimized for memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: An x86_64 virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio of eight gigabytes per dedicated AMD vCPU with remote storage support.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD processor with x86_64 architecture
Features 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory
Provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU
Premium Storage capable with no local storage included
